summary.intent: Address Greptile code review feedback on PR #805 for the legacy SSE transport in the moltis MCP crate.
summary.outcome: Fixed all three Greptile-flagged issues (P1 blocking SSE read, TOCTOU race in get_message_url, fragile endpoint heuristic), updated affected tests, and committed the changes. All 108 tests pass.

repo: The moltis workspace uses futures 0.3 for async streaming utilities.
Greptile is integrated as a PR review bot and posts inline comments plus a confidence score (3/5 in this case).
The MCP crate has a legacy_sse_transport.rs with SSE client logic including endpoint URL discovery.

code.finding: resp.text().await was blocking on open SSE streams — must use incremental/streaming reads instead.
get_message_url had a TOCTOU race; fixed with a double-checked write pattern.
Endpoint URL detection used a fragile heuristic; replaced with strict requirement for an explicit 'event: endpoint' SSE line. One test was updated to reflect the new stricter behavior.

workflow: Greptile review results are available via PR reviews, inline comments, and check run output — check all three sources to get the full picture.
After fixing Greptile feedback, run clippy + tests + rustfmt before committing to keep CI clean.

friction: Greptile confidence score required checking multiple sources (PR body, issue comments, check run output) before being found.
The heuristic removal broke an existing test that had to be manually identified and updated.
open_items: No explicit open items deferred in this session; the three Greptile issues were fully resolved.
